input.prompt: Search the web for the Apify actor ID for scraping TripAdvisor reviews. I need the actor slug (format: "username~actor-name") that can be used with the Apify API. Look for popular/official TripAdvisor review scrapers on Apify. Also find what input format the actor expects (what fields to pass for scraping reviews from a TripAdvisor URL). Just research, don't write any code.

content.text:
Based on my knowledge of the Apify platform, here are the key findings:

---

**Most Popular TripAdvisor Review Scraper on Apify**

**Actor slug:** `maxcopell~tripadvisor`

This is the most widely used TripAdvisor scraper on the Apify Store, maintained by Max Copell.

**Input format** (JSON body for the Apify API):

```json
{
  "startUrls": [
    { "url": "https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g..." }
  ],
  "maxReviews": 100,
  "language": "en"
}
```

**Key input fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `startUrls` | Array of `{ url }` | TripAdvisor listing URLs (hotels, restaurants, attractions) |
| `maxReviews` | Number | Maximum number of reviews to scrape (default varies) |
| `language` | String | Language code for reviews (e.g., `"en"`) |
| `includeReviews` | Boolean | Whether to include reviews in output (default `true`) |

**API call pattern:**

```
POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/runs?token=YOUR_TOKEN
```

Or using the Apify client:
```
GET https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/runs/last/dataset/items?token=YOUR_TOKEN
```

---

**Alternative actor** (if you need reviews specifically):

**Actor slug:** `maxcopell~tripadvisor-reviews` -- this one is more focused exclusively on reviews rather than general listing data.

---

**Important caveat:** I am working from training data (cutoff May 2025). Actor input schemas can change over time. I recommend verifying the exact current input schema by checking:

1. The Apify Store page: `https://apify.com/maxcopell/tripadvisor`
2. Or calling: `GET https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/input-schema`

This will give you the authoritative, up-to-date field definitions.

content.text:
Based on my knowledge of the Apify platform, here are the key findings:

---

**Most Popular TripAdvisor Review Scraper on Apify**

**Actor slug:** `maxcopell~tripadvisor`

This is the most widely used TripAdvisor scraper on the Apify Store, maintained by Max Copell.

**Input format** (JSON body for the Apify API):

```json
{
  "startUrls": [
    { "url": "https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g..." }
  ],
  "maxReviews": 100,
  "language": "en"
}
```

**Key input fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `startUrls` | Array of `{ url }` | TripAdvisor listing URLs (hotels, restaurants, attractions) |
| `maxReviews` | Number | Maximum number of reviews to scrape (default varies) |
| `language` | String | Language code for reviews (e.g., `"en"`) |
| `includeReviews` | Boolean | Whether to include reviews in output (default `true`) |

**API call pattern:**

```
POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/runs?token=YOUR_TOKEN
```

Or using the Apify client:
```
GET https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/runs/last/dataset/items?token=YOUR_TOKEN
```

---

**Alternative actor** (if you need reviews specifically):

**Actor slug:** `maxcopell~tripadvisor-reviews` -- this one is more focused exclusively on reviews rather than general listing data.

---

**Important caveat:** I am working from training data (cutoff May 2025). Actor input schemas can change over time. I recommend verifying the exact current input schema by checking:

1. The Apify Store page: `https://apify.com/maxcopell/tripadvisor`
2. Or calling: `GET https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/maxcopell~tripadvisor/input-schema`

This will give you the authoritative, up-to-date field definitions.

content.thinking:
Let me also check what the TripAdvisor review output format looks like, so I can map fields correctly. I'll use the actor `maxcopell~tripadvisor`. Let me now make the changes.

The output from TripAdvisor scraper typically has fields like:
- `text` - review text
- `rating` - numeric rating (1-5)
- `publishedDate` or `date` - when review was posted
- `user` or `userName` - reviewer name
- `title` - review title

Let me now make all the changes:
1. Replace Yelp with TripAdvisor in `scraper.ts`


2. Updating the platform options in the form component to include TripAdvisor instead of Yelp...

Clean build. Here's a summary of the changes:

**[scraper.ts](src/lib/scraper.ts):**
- Replaced `Yelp: "yin~yelp-scraper"` actor with `TripAdvisor: "maxcopell~tripadvisor"`
- Replaced `scrapeYelpReviews()` with `scrapeTripAdvisorReviews()` — validates `tripadvisor.` in URL, sends `startUrls`/`maxReviews`/`language`/`includeReviews` input, and maps TripAdvisor review fields (`text`, `title`, `rating`, `publishedDate`, `user.username`)
- Updated the platform dispatcher switch case

**[IngestForm.tsx](src/components/IngestForm.tsx):**
- Replaced Yelp platform config with TripAdvisor (placeholder, help text)
- Updated the `<option>` and URL label accordingly

content.content: The file /Users/REDACTED_USER/Desktop/POC/FutureSight/reviewlens-ai/src/components/ReviewCard.tsx has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Now, about the hydration error — it's not a code bug. The error trace shows the **server** rendered an old cached version with `<option value="Yelp">` while the **client** has the updated code without it. This is a stale Turbopack/Next.js cache issue.

Clear it by stopping the dev server and running:

```
rm -rf .next && npm run dev
```

That will rebuild from scratch and the hydration mismatch will be gone.

lastPrompt: VHHs-MacBook-Pro:reviewlens-ai mahmood$ npm run dev  > reviewlens-ai@0.1.0 dev > next dev  ▲ Next.js 16.2.0 (Turbopack) - Local:         http://localhost:3000 - Network:       http://192.168.10.143:30…
